entries.section: Leveling Tips for Balance Druid DPS in TBC Classic
entries.text: Make sure you know when to use your utility. For example, only affects Melee and Ranged Hit, not Spell Hit. If you are solo questing or in a group with mainly casters, this debuff isn't necessary. However, if you are with people who can benefit from it, this debuff goes a long way for their DPS and will speed up your progress.

entries.section: Leveling Tips for Balance Druid DPS in TBC Classic
entries.text: Keep a stock of water on you at all times. You will need a lot of water with you no matter what content you are doing. Try to keep a healthy supply; otherwise, you'll be scraping for mana more than you'd like, which will end up wasting a good amount of time.

entries.section: Leveling Tips for Balance Druid DPS in TBC Classic
entries.text: Make friends in the groups you join. Since finding groups for dungeons can be time-consuming, try to befriend other players who are doing the same content as you, and you are likely to be invited to more dungeons or other activities.

entries.section: Leveling Tips for Balance Druid DPS in TBC Classic
entries.text: While you don't need to venture back to the trainer each time you level up, there are a few key levels where you get access to new forms. At level 10, you'll gain access to Bear Form , which is a huge boost to survival. At level 20, you'll gain Cat Form . At level 30, you can learn Travel Form , which will greatly reduce travel time until you can get a mount at level 40. At level 40, you gain . And finally, at level 68, you can train Travel Form a whole two levels before other classes can buy a flying mount.
